actionUnits.condition: Attempt to manually invoke Interactable UnityEvent(s) configured via MonoBehaviour.m_Calls on --INTERACTABLE-- or its children when specific conditions are met. In the provided scene excerpt for fileID 1782057475, there is no MonoBehaviour component (no m_Calls), Collider, or EventTrigger shown; Has_Rigidbody is False, so Grab/Transform are not applicable. To proceed, please provide the MonoBehaviour component(s) (script fileID, GUID, public no-arg methods) and any EventTrigger/Collider present on this object or its children so we can specify exact triggerring_events.

This manual trigger replicates the XR 'Activate' to ensure events fire without controller input.

Attempt to exceed velocity thresholds to invoke Candle OnVelocity.OnBegin UnityEvent. Scene MonoBehaviour_1 on Candle (id: 1713785677, guid: 2eb756a4b82b24040a468c0a73063e4d) has beginThreshold 1.25 and OnBegin.m_PersistentCalls calling ParticleSystem.Stop on fileID 2038875. Note: this component is disabled (m_Enabled: 0); if not enabled at runtime, the event will not fire. If enabled, moving the Candle quickly should trigger OnBegin and stop the flame particles.

Manually exercise audio control methods on PlaySoundsFromList to cover playback paths. XRGrabInteractable (MonoBehaviour_0 id: 1768355684, guid: 8a35f6cfbfba9b548aaa00d52cfe8a50) is attached on Light_Switch. Its UnityEvents (m_Calls) are configured in scene meta: SelectEntered invokes ToggleOnOff.Toggle on two components (fileID: 1887208566 and 2061883187) and also SetActive(false) on a target GameObject (fileID: 377328966). HoverEntered/Exited invoke SetActive(true/false) on that same GameObject. Per Trigger action constraints, only public no-arg methods on MonoBehaviours can be invoked directly; thus we manually invoke both Toggle() calls to cover SelectEntered’s script paths.

XRGrabInteractable (id: 1768355684) HoverEntered/Exited UnityEvents call GameObject.SetActive(true/false) on fileID 377328966. These require a bool parameter and target a GameObject, which cannot be invoked via the Trigger action (only supports no-arg MonoBehaviour methods). Provide a no-arg wrapper MonoBehaviour on that GameObject (e.g., IndicatorProxy.Enable()/Disable()) or confirm an alternative callable no-arg method so we can include them; otherwise these two event calls can only be validated through actual XR hover.
